title: On-demand mini:decision + thin trigger
goal: Move the full ADR drafting instruction out of the done prompt into a new on-demand 'mini context decision' command (add 'decision' to CONTEXT_COMMANDS) with a matching /mini:decision slash command; insert into the do/auto prompt only a sharp, apt, concise trigger that offers running /mini:decision when the phase hits a real crossroads; slim the heavy ADR block out of buildDoneSessionPrompt. Acceptance: the trigger must stay sharp enough not to raise the rate of forgotten ADRs.

steps.title: buildDecisionSessionPrompt + mini context decision
steps.detail: New builder in sessionContext.ts holding the full ADR drafting instruction (draft -> show user -> mini decision --apply), lifted from the done block. Add 'decision' to CONTEXT_COMMANDS and route it through buildPhaseContext to the new builder. Update context.test.ts (CONTEXT_COMMANDS array) and add a test/snapshot for the new builder.

steps.title: Slim buildDoneSessionPrompt to a thin pointer
steps.detail: Remove the ~37-line ADR block, replace with a ~2-line conditional pointer to /mini:decision (run it before /mini:done; skip if already recorded). Update the done snapshot test.

steps.title: Thin pointer in the do prompt (buildAutoPhasePrompt)
steps.detail: ~2 sharp lines near the report section: on a real crossroads, at the end tell the user to run /mini:decision before /mini:done -- do not write it yourself. Update the autoPhase snapshot test.

steps.title: Register /mini:decision in COMMAND_DEFS
steps.detail: New entry with the default thin body (runs mini context decision). Update the install-commands test.

steps.title: Docs + README
steps.detail: Add docs/interactive/decision.md; mention /mini:decision in the README slash-command list.

resolvedVerify: Are the two thin ADR pointers (do + done) sharp enough?
